_comment: Backend binary selection -- which llama-server build to launch for THIS machine, keyed by (OS, GPU vendor). Variable-first cross-platform prep: v1 ships ONE working entry (Windows + NVIDIA -> the CUDA build at bin/llama-server.exe); the other rows are forward-looking placeholders so v2.0 platform adoption is 'drop in a binary + flip a row here', not code surgery. The resolver (config.py -> resolve_binary_path) falls back to the built-in Windows/NVIDIA default if this file is missing or malformed, so a bad edit can never brick launch. Pairs with the platform-layer seam; see the cross-platform section of the project design notes.

os_binary_suffix._comment: Executable filename suffix per OS, appended to a backend's 'binary' base name. Windows needs .exe; POSIX has none. Unlisted OS -> resolver defaults to .exe on Windows, '' elsewhere.

backends._comment: A backend = one llama-server build. 'binary' is the base name (the OS suffix above is appended); 'subdir' is relative to runtime.json -> bin_dir ('' = the bin/ root, which is where v1 ships the flat CUDA build). To add a build: drop its binary in and add a row here + a matrix cell below. 'status' is documentation only (the code never reads it).

cuda.label: NVIDIA CUDA
cuda.notes: The v1 build. Windows + NVIDIA -> bin/llama-server.exe. Linux + NVIDIA reuses it (bin/llama-server) once a Linux CUDA build is dropped in.

vulkan.label: Vulkan (AMD / Intel)
vulkan.notes: Cross-vendor GPU offload for AMD + Intel. Not shipped in v1; the subdir keeps it from colliding with the CUDA build.

metal.label: Apple Metal
metal.notes: macOS on Apple Silicon. The unified-memory budget path is a separate v2 item (it breaks the VRAM/RAM split model).

cpu.label: CPU-only
cpu.notes: No-GPU fallback build. Not shipped in v1.

matrix._comment: OS -> (GPU vendor -> backend id). Vendor strings match platform_layer._vendor_from_name: 'NVIDIA' / 'AMD' / 'Intel' / 'Unknown'. '_default' applies when the probed vendor isn't listed OR when the binary is resolved without a GPU probe (e.g. the Setup doctor, which shouldn't probe just to locate a file). v1 only ever exercises Windows/NVIDIA -> cuda; every other cell is a v2.0 placeholder.
